note_data.title: Natural Resources
keyPoints: Pakistan has **abundant water resources** from rivers Indus, Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi and Sutlej which provide irrigation and hydroelectric power
The **Indus Basin Irrigation System** is the world's largest contiguous irrigation network covering 16 million hectares
Major **dams** include Tarbela (world's largest earth-filled dam), Mangla and Warsak which provide water storage and electricity generation
Pakistan has only **4% forest cover** which is below the recommended 25%, leading to soil erosion and flooding
Important **mineral resources** include natural gas (Sui), coal (Thar), rock salt (Khewra), limestone and iron ore
**Natural gas** provides 50% of Pakistan's energy needs and is found mainly in Balochistan and Sindh
**Fishing industry** provides employment and protein, with major fishing ports at Karachi and Gwadar
**Deforestation** causes soil erosion, floods, loss of wildlife habitat and reduced rainfall
**Water scarcity** affects agriculture due to inefficient irrigation methods, canal seepage and population growth

definitions.definition: Materials found in nature that can be used by humans for economic benefit
The artificial application of water to crops through canals, tube wells and other methods
The permanent removal of forest cover for agriculture, urbanization or commercial purposes
Electricity generated by using flowing water to turn turbines in dams

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give characteristics or features - what it looks like or what happens
Give practical solutions with brief justification

modelAnswer.question: Explain why forests are important natural resources for Pakistan. [6 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: Forests prevent soil erosion because tree roots bind the soil together. They reduce flooding because trees absorb rainwater and slow surface runoff. Forests provide timber for construction and furniture industry which creates employment. They are habitats for wildlife which maintains biodiversity. Trees produce oxygen and absorb carbon dioxide which improves air quality. Forests attract rainfall because they release water vapor through transpiration.

commonMistakes: Never say 'forests give us wood' - always say 'forests provide timber for construction and furniture industry'
Students often forget to explain HOW forests prevent flooding - must mention water absorption
Don't just list minerals - explain their economic importance and location
Never say 'Pakistan has many rivers' - name specific rivers like Indus, Jhelum, Chenab
Students confuse canal irrigation with tube wells - be specific about irrigation methods
examinerTips: Natural resources questions appear in every Paper 2 - focus on economic importance
Always give Pakistani examples - Tarbela Dam, Khewra Salt Mines, Sui gas fields
For 6-mark questions, give 3 points with explanations rather than 6 simple points
Maps may ask you to mark resource locations - know major dams, gas fields and forests
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Map of Pakistan showing major dams (Tarbela, Mangla, Warsak), gas fields (Sui, Qadirpur), forest areas (northern mountains, Changa Manga), and main rivers of Indus system
